decks.notes: The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts and efficient creatures to quickly overwhelm the opponent, while also disrupting their game plan with counterspells and graveyard hate. By utilizing cards like Mox Opal, Engineered Explosives, and Urza's Saga, as well as creatures like Arcbound Ravager and Emry, Lurker of the Loch, the deck aims to establish a strong board presence and deal damage quickly. The inclusion of cards like Tormod's Crypt and Pithing Needle also gives the deck the ability to disrupt graveyard and activated abilities strategies, making it a versatile and aggressive deck in the Modern format.

The deck focuses on maintaining control over the game through a combination of removal spells, counterspells, and powerful creatures. It uses cards like Flame of Anor and Wrath of the Skies to control the board, while also utilizing Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury to generate card advantage and maintain pressure on the opponent. The deck also includes cards like Fable of the Mirror-Breaker and Isochron Scepter to create a versatile and adaptable game plan. With a mix of control elements and win conditions, the deck aims to outlast the opponent and secure victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing powerful control spells like Fatal Push, Spell Pierce, and Drown in the Loch to disrupt the opponent's game plan while deploying threats like Murktide Regent and Dragon's Rage Channeler to close out the game. The deck also includes card selection and advantage engines like Preordain, Expressive Iteration, and Mishra's Bauble to ensure consistent draws and progression towards victory. With a mix of removal, countermagic, and threats, the Grixis Control deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver its opponent in a long game of attrition.

The main focus of this UW control deck is to efficiently control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy using a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage engines like Narset, Parter of Veils. The deck also includes ways to generate card advantage through Consult the Star Charts and Geier Reach Sanitarium, while utilizing powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Time Raveler to further dominate the game. It aims to outlast opponents through a combination of card advantage, disruption, and powerful win conditions like Wrath of the Skies and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on utilizing aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to quickly overwhelm the opponent. It also includes burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment to deal direct damage and control the board. The deck includes cards like Arena of Glory and Voice of Victory to provide additional support for the aggressive game plan. The inclusion of cards like Ranger-Captain of Eos and Seasoned Pyromancer allows for card advantage and flexibility in different situations. The deck aims to win by early pressure and efficient removal spells.

The deck focuses on utilizing creatures with cycling abilities to manipulate the graveyard and then cast Living End to bring all of those creatures back onto the battlefield. By cycling through cards like Wistfulness and Street Wraith, the player sets up a powerful board state with creatures like Overlord of the Balemurk and Shardless Agent. With the addition of control elements like Force of Negation and Subtlety, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while assembling its combo pieces for a big finish.

This deck focuses on casting multiple spells in a single turn to generate a large amount of mana and then use that mana to cast a lethal grape shot. The key strategy is to chain together rituals, cantrips, and other spells to dig through the deck and find the pieces needed to combo off, while using Ral, Monsoon Mage to double the effect of instants and sorceries. The deck aims to quickly assemble the combo pieces and win the game before the opponent has a chance to respond.

The main idea of this deck is to control the game by denying resources to the opponent and disrupting their game plan. By utilizing cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Thought-Knot Seer, the deck's goal is to slow down the opponent's strategy and gain an advantage. The deck also looks to generate massive amounts of mana using the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Mine, Urza's Tower, and Urza's Power Plant) to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures like Sire of Seven Deaths and Glaring Fleshraker. Ultimately, the deck aims to establish board control and overwhelm the opponent with its powerful threats.

The deck focuses on quickly assembling a powerful board presence by leveraging the synergy between creatures like Allosaurus Rider and powerful spells like Eldritch Evolution and Neoform. By sacrificing creatures to tutor for even larger threats like Griselbrand and Borborygmos Enraged, the deck can quickly overwhelm opponents with its combo potential. The inclusion of counter spells like Pact of Negation and protection spells like Veil of Summer allow the deck to protect its key pieces and ensure its combo can go off uninterrupted. Additionally, cards like Endurance and Ghalta, Stampede Tyrant provide additional utility and resilience to the deck's game plan.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with powerful creatures using cards like Faithless Looting and Otherworldly Gaze, then reanimate them with spells like Persist and Unearth. This combo reanimator strategy focuses on getting big threats like Archon of Cruelty and Emperor of Bones onto the battlefield early to overwhelm the opponent. By combining graveyard manipulation and efficient reanimation spells, this deck aims to win the game through powerful creature recursion and aggressive plays.

The deck focuses on quickly reanimating powerful creatures from the graveyard using cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Faithful Mending, while also disrupting opponents with counterspells like Force of Negation. The deck also utilizes blink effects like Ephemerate to protect creatures and generate additional value. The main win condition is to cheat out creatures like Griselbrand or Atraxa, Grand Unifier early in the game to overwhelm the opponent. The deck also includes a mix of removal spells like Prismatic Ending and Thoughtseize to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly establishing a board presence and applying pressure on the opponent. It utilizes a mix of low-cost creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, burn spells like Galvanic Discharge, and powerful enchantments like Arena of Glory to overwhelm the opponent. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Ranger-Captain of Eos and Thraben Charm to control the board and protect its creatures. With a focus on speed and aggression, this deck aims to quickly take control of the game and close out matches before the opponent has a chance to stabilize.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive strategies, utilizing powerful creatures like Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer to quickly overwhelm opponents. The deck also includes removal spells like Path to Exile and Galvanic Discharge to control the board. Additionally, cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Goblin Bombardment provide ways to deal direct damage to the opponent. The mana base is built around fetch lands like Arid Mesa and Flooded Strand to ensure consistent access to the necessary colors. Overall, the deck aims to apply pressure early and maintain a strong board presence throughout the game.

This deck focuses on controlling the opponent's draws and resources using Lantern of Insight and Codex Shredder. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Inquisition of Kozilek and Thoughtseize, while locking them out of attacking using Ensnaring Bridge. Blood Moon disrupts mana bases, while Urza's Saga generates value. The deck also uses The Underworld Cookbook and Molten Collapse for additional disruption. Overall, the key concept of this deck is to control the game by limiting the opponent's options and resources, while maintaining a strong defensive position.

The deck focuses on playing aggressively while utilizing blink effects to gain extra value from creatures and spells. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and powerful spells like Consign to Memory and Galvanic Discharge. By utilizing cards like Ephemerate and Quantum Riddler, the deck can protect its key threats and generate additional card advantage. The inclusion of cards like Solitude and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury provides removal options and additional threats to close out the game quickly.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with high-cost creatures and then reanimate them using spells like Unearth and Persist. By utilizing cards like Thought Scour and Faithless Looting to fill the graveyard, the deck is able to bring out powerful creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Abhorrent Oculus early in the game. The inclusion of control spells like Spell Pierce and Fatal Push help to protect the combo pieces while disrupting the opponent's game plan. Overall, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with explosive plays and powerful creatures from the graveyard.
